slides.title: Arcadia makes capital follow proof.
slides.claim: A Solana vault marketplace where traders earn allocation only after proving performance with their own first-loss capital.
bullets: Investors see public risk buffers before depositing
slides.proof: Live app: marketplace, vault detail, manager console, trade terminal.

slides.title: Managed DeFi vaults still run on trust.
slides.claim: Investors are asked to fund managers before seeing enough proof, while managers keep upside without enough downside.
bullets: No required skin in the game
No enforced track record before deposits
slides.proof: Arcadia turns those social assumptions into protocol checks.

slides.title: Solana rails make proof-gated vaults practical.
slides.claim: Fast settlement, public program state, Pyth prices, and Jupiter routing let Arcadia enforce risk controls in the product loop.
bullets: Routing support can unwind exposure when liquidity is short
slides.proof: No off-chain spreadsheet needs to decide whether an investor can exit.

slides.title: A marketplace for traders who prove first.
slides.claim: Investors browse graduated vaults by buffer, performance, health, and strategy instead of trusting a pitch.
bullets: Portfolio and alerts built around withdrawal confidence
slides.proof: The marketplace is designed as the first product surface, not a marketing page.

slides.title: The trader’s money is the first fuse.
slides.claim: Junior capital protects senior investor capital and shrinks trading freedom as the buffer deteriorates.
bullets: Dynamic position limits as health falls
slides.proof: The manager UI shows progress, capital, status, and limits as operating constraints.

slides.title: One flow, two roles, strict boundaries.
slides.claim: Arcadia separates investor discovery from trader operations so each role sees the right controls.
bullets: Manager console to trade limits and operations
slides.proof: Investor views do not expose execution controls; trader views focus on vault management.

slides.title: Withdrawal safety is part of accounting, not support.
slides.claim: The v1 model uses USDC base liquidity and WSOL exposure so withdrawals can be valued and protected.
slides.proof: Shares were removed from the user model; investor data now tracks principal and claim.

slides.title: Arcadia is stronger because it is on-chain.
slides.claim: The product relies on public state, non-custodial custody, composable vault records, and deterministic execution paths.
slides.proof: The program, generated clients, frontend, and indexer are designed as one Solana-native loop.

slides.title: The MVP is already a real product surface.
slides.claim: Arcadia has a polished frontend, role-gated flows, program design, indexer scaffold, and tests around core accounting behavior.
bullets: Arcadia UI and SVG brand system
USDC/WSOL accounting plan implemented in program direction
Frontend tests/build/lint passing before media work
slides.proof: The demo package captures the app directly from localhost.

slides.title: Help Arcadia recruit the first serious managers.
slides.claim: Colosseum support helps move Arcadia from MVP to pilot vaults with disciplined traders and real feedback.
bullets: Select Arcadia for the Solana DeFi track
Introduce pilot traders and protocol reviewers
slides.proof: The next milestone is not more hype. It is guarded live capital with visible proof.
